rulings.Case Title: GEORGE LUCAS,JR et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Monetary Sanctions Against Brayton Purcell For Misuse Of The Discovery Process
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, July 19, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Aerojet-General Corp.¿s motion for monetary sanctions against Brayton Purcell for misuse of the discovery process: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Depositions Of The Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Records Of Defendant Douglass Insulation Company, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, July 19, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ amended motion to compel the depositions of the person most qualified and custodian of records of defendant Douglass Insulation Co., Inc. and to produce documents is granted provided that plaintiffs furnish to defendant Mr. Patterson¿s job sites between 1961 and 1975. Defendant is only required to produce documents for those job sites that plaintiff worked at. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied because defendant acted with substantial justification to oppose plaintiffs¿ motion. Defendant¿s opposition was timely filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE PATTERSON et al VS. ALTA BUILDING MATERIALS CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Depositions Of The Person Most Qualified And Custodian Of Records Of Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Thursday, July 19, 2012 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel the depositions of the person most qualified and custodian of records of defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc. and production of documents is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiffs¿ motion is denied as to production of a deponent. Defendant is under no obligation to produce a deponent. (Maldonado v. Superior Court (2002) 94 Cal.App.4th 1390.) Plaintiffs¿ motion is granted as to production of documents, insofar as any exist. Plaintiffs¿ request for sanctions is denied. Defendant¿s opposition was timely filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30am, not 9:00am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
